title: Lastrea macgregorii
taxonomy: Lastrea macgregorii is a species of fern in the family Athyriaceae. It is a type of lady fern that is native to the tropical forests of Asia. The classification of Lastrea macgregorii is as follows: kingdom Plantae, phylum Pteridophyta, class Polypodiopsida, order Polypodiales, family Athyriaceae, genus Lastrea, and species L. macgregorii. This fern is characterized by its lacy fronds and delicate appearance. The classification of Lastrea macgregorii reflects its unique characteristics and evolutionary history as a distinct species within the Athyriaceae family.
uses: Lastrea macgregorii is primarily found in Australia and New Zealand. However, information about its medicinal uses is limited.
